key_insights.insight: Looking for perfection in others leads to disappointment; instead, appreciate people for the specific qualities they bring to your life.
key_insights.quote: One of the biggest mistakes I've made and I think we make as humans is we often look for divinity in humanity...And when you seek divinity in humanity, you're left with insecurity and anxiety because no one fulfills that divine search.

key_insights.insight: Relationships can be understood through four pillars: care, competence, consistency, and character; individuals will often fulfill some, but rarely all, of these roles.
key_insights.quote: There were four pillars of relationships and they are care, competence, consistency, and character. Every single person in your life is going to be able to give you or should be able to give you at least one of these four characteristics.

key_insights.insight: It is important to appreciate people for what they bring into your life rather than focusing on what they lack.
key_insights.quote: We're always looking for which C they don't have rather than appreciating for them for exactly what they bring to our life.

key_insights.insight: True love and partnership involve being valued for who you are, not just for external achievements or ambitions.
key_insights.quote: My wife loved me for everything that came before that. She loves me despite all of that. If all of that was to go away tomorrow, she'd still love me.

key_insights.insight: Pay attention to what your partner truly values in you, as it reflects their values and what they find meaningful.
key_insights.quote: It is beautiful that you get an opportunity to learn about your partner's values by what they value in your own success.

actionable_advice.advice: Identify individuals in your life and categorize them based on the four pillars (care, competence, consistency, character) to better understand and appreciate their role.
actionable_advice.how_to: Make a list of people in your life. Beside each name, note which of the four qualities they consistently demonstrate. Recognize that no one will fulfill all categories, and appreciate their unique contribution.

actionable_advice.advice: When feeling annoyed by your partner, consider it an opportunity to learn and grow.
actionable_advice.how_to: Instead of reacting negatively, pause and ask yourself what lesson you can learn from the situation or perspective your partner is offering.

memorable_stories: The guest shared an anecdote about his wife not being impressed by his external successes, which initially frustrated him but ultimately taught him the importance of being loved for who he is.
The guest mentions how his wife 'steals' all his friends because of her amazing personality.
contrarian_claims: The idea that needing validation for success from a partner can be a sign of valuing the wrong things.
Society often encourages us to value external achievements, but finding a partner who values something deeper can lead to a more fulfilling relationship.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 83 - Jay Shetty's 4 Pillars for Long Lasting Relationships
